rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes for port-level contracts, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use visible feature names and concrete bounds when defining range assumptions and guarantees in component types.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid AADL scopes to maintain architectural integrity.
Define range constraints using visible identifiers and named constants for clarity and traceability.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use boolean expressions to define range conditions in AGREE: eq var : bool = x >= min and x <= max;
Component type contracts should reference port features through valid variables or outputs, not subcomponent ports directly.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes instead.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
